subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to identify publishers with declining quarterly spend trends in 2024 (Q1 > Q2 > Q3 > Q4 or any downward multi-quarter pattern). Return publisher ID, name, and quarterly spend breakdown.

For each publisher with declining trend, generate renewal recommendation (hold, expand, reduce) by analyzing trend severity, spend history, contract status, and strategic value. Classify each publisher's posture and output structured recommendation.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid-tier) to fetch publishers with declining Q1-Q4 trends in 2024 using window functions and conditional aggregation—mid complexity due to multi-quarter comparison and filtering logic. (2) cross_recon (mid-tier) to reconcile the declining trend data with contract/spend context and generate renewal posture recommendations—this is classification and recommendation generation, which is cross_recon's scope. No separate policy_action needed since governance evaluation was not requested. No multitool_plan needed. Minimal decomposition: one fetch step, one recommendation step.

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "WITH quarterly_spend AS (
  SELECT
    publisher,
    SUM(CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 1 THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS q1_spend,
    SUM(CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 2 THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS q2_spend,
    SUM(CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 3 THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS q3_spend,
    SUM(CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 4 THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS q4_spend,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 1 THEN fiscal_quarter END) AS has_q1,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 2 THEN fiscal_quarter END) AS has_q2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 3 THEN fiscal_quarter END) AS has_q3,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN fiscal_quarter = 4 THEN fiscal_quarter END) AS has_q4
  FROM consumption_facts
  WHERE event_date >= '2024-01-01'
    AND event_date < '2025-01-01'
  GROUP BY publisher
),
decline_analysis AS (
  SELECT
    publisher,
    q1_spend,
    q2_spend,
    q3_spend,
    q4_spend,
    -- Count how many consecutive downward steps exist across available quarters
    (
      CASE WHEN has_q1 = 1 AND has_q2 = 1 AND q2_spend < q1_spend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
      CASE WHEN has_q2 = 1 AND has_q3 = 1 AND q3_spend < q2_spend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
      CASE WHEN has_q3 = 1 AND has_q4 = 1 AND q4_spend < q3_spend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) AS declining_steps,
    (
      CASE WHEN has_q1 = 1 AND has_q2 = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
      CASE WHEN has_q2 = 1 AND has_q3 = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
      CASE WHEN has_q3 = 1 AND has_q4 = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) AS comparable_steps,
    -- Overall spend trend: last available quarter vs first available quarter
    CASE
      WHEN has_q4 = 1 AND has_q1 = 1 THEN q4_spend - q1_spend
      WHEN has_q3 = 1 AND has_q1 = 1 THEN q3_spend - q1_spend
      WHEN has_q2 = 1 AND has_q1 = 1 THEN q2_
